payload.text:
상세 설정에 카페 상세 설정에서 365일 운영하는게 기준인데 매출/회전율 예측 카드에 옵션을 주고싶어.
365일 운영할 때, 주에 5일 운영할 때, 주 6일 운영할 때를 옵션으로 만들어줘.

**Revising Prediction Options**

I've been meticulously examining the `PlannerTab`'s "Sales/Turnover Prediction" card. My focus shifted to enriching its functionality. I'm actively integrating an additional configuration option within the "Cafe Detailed Settings" section. This will be an enhancement to the existing parameters of `seatCount`, `operatingHours`, `stayDuration`, `turnoverTarget`, and `operatingDays`. My goal is to make these prediction settings as comprehensive and adaptable as possible.


**Enhancing Day Configuration**

I'm now zeroing in on the `operatingDays` setting within the `Sales/Turnover Prediction` card. Currently, this appears as a number in the backend data. I'm considering refactoring this to use a preset selection, like "365 days," "5 days a week," or "6 days a week". This adjustment aims to simplify user input and make the prediction logic more intuitive. The calculation should translate these selections into an average monthly representation for use with the monthly data format.


**Defining Day Presets**

I'm translating the user's specific requests for `operatingDays` configurations into concrete presets. I've been working on a new UI component, likely a dropdown, to allow users to select from "365 days," "5 days a week," and "6 days a week."  I've mapped these choices to the correct monthly representation in the backend by hardcoding the expected monthly operating days: 30.4 for 365 days, 22 for 5 days a week, and 26 for 6 days a week. Next, I plan to integrate these changes within the `types.ts` file to properly implement the configuration.


**Integrating Operating Days**

I'm now focusing on integrating the "Operation Schedule" selector within the "Sales/Turnover Prediction" card in `PlannerTab.tsx`. I've decided buttons would provide a cleaner interface than a dropdown.  My choices for operating days will be: "365일 (연중무휴)", "주 6일", and "주 5일".  These map directly to 30.4, 26, and 22 days per month respectively, ensuring simplicity and accuracy in monthly financial projections. I'll update `config.cafe.operatingDays` with the selected value.


**Refining Day Selector UI**

I'm now focusing on crafting the UI for the operating days selector. I'll probably inline the UI within the existing code to keep it streamlined. It'll integrate directly into the existing card, alongside the other input fields. I'm keen to make the integration seamless, ensuring an intuitive user experience for these core parameters.


**Implementing New Selector**

I've just added the UI for the day selector, and I'm updating `PlannerTab.tsx` to integrate it. The new component uses buttons instead of a dropdown, and is inlined for streamlined look. I've also updated the `config.cafe.operatingDays` value upon selection to ensure accurate monthly projections.


**Revising Day Calculation**

I'm now revising the calculations for the operating day presets to align with user needs, focusing on accuracy and simplicity.  I'll translate "연중무휴," "주 6일," and "주 5일" to 30.4, 26.1, and 21.7 days per month, respectively, for the monthly predictions. This update ensures precise financial projections. Next, I'll update the UI implementation in `PlannerTab.tsx` to reflect these revised numerical presets.


**Adding Operating Days Input**

I'm now incorporating an input for `operatingDays` directly into the "Cafe" settings within `PlannerTab.tsx`, below `stayDuration`. This will align the Cafe settings with Space and Wine, and make the prediction logic more explicit and user-controllable. I'll need to remember to expose `operatingDays` and its updated value for calculations, specifically in `App.tsx`'s `calculateFinancials` function, which currently uses a default value of 26.


**Updating the UI Element**

I'm presently refactoring the user interface for the operating days selector, focusing on the Cafe Detailed Settings section within the `PlannerTab.tsx` file. I'm moving away from buttons toward a more intuitive segmented control to enhance the user experience. The control will provide direct input for selecting operating days per month and I've integrated this into the `PlannerTab`'s Cafe settings for the Sales/Turnover Prediction. I will integrate this with the existing configuration management system.


**Optimizing Day Selection**

I'm now revising the UI element for the operating days. I've switched from buttons to a segmented control, enhancing user experience. I've integrated this into the cafe settings.  The focus is on accuracy, so I'm now optimizing the selection thresholds for the "active" state of each option: 21.7, 26.0, and 30.4 days per month for 5 days/week, 6 days/week, and 365 days/year, respectively. I will be storing standard decimals.


**Integrating Korean Labels**

I'm now focusing on integrating Korean labels for the segmented control within `PlannerTab.tsx`. The options will be "주 5일" (5 days a week), "주 6일" (6 days a week), and "연중무휴" (everyday). I'm placing this control inside the existing `div` element, as planned. I've confirmed that `operatingDays` is correctly defined in `types.ts`, so I can now proceed with the implementation in the designated `renderCafeDetailPlanner` section.


**Fine-tuning Input Fields**

I'm now putting the finishing touches on the operating days configuration within the Cafe settings. I've successfully implemented the segmented control with the Korean labels. Accuracy is paramount, so the preset values will be 21.7, 26.1, and 30.4 days per month. I've also incorporated the necessary code to handle the selection change. The UI and backend now work flawlessly to deliver precise monthly financial projections based on the selected operating schedule.


**Confirming Data Integration**

I'm verifying that the `operatingDays` value from the segmented control in the Cafe settings is correctly integrated with the existing number inputs in the `renderCafeDetailPlanner` section. Specifically, the display of the operating days value. I've located the appropriate insertion point and now the UI will accurately reflect the chosen operating schedule from the control. This ensures all calculation dependencies are met.


**Implementing Operating Days Selector**

I'm now integrating the operating days selector above the existing "operatingHours" input field within the Cafe settings. I'll need to remember to correctly add this to the Sales/Turnover Prediction card. I'm focusing on ensuring its functionality while maintaining the component's intuitive design. I will use the established components like `NumberInput` and `SliderInput`, imported from `inputs` in `PlannerTab.tsx`, ensuring consistency.


**Implementing Operating Days Selector**

I'm now integrating the operating days selector above the existing "operatingHours" input field within the Cafe settings. I'll need to remember to correctly add this to the Sales/Turnover Prediction card. I'm focusing on ensuring its functionality while maintaining the component's intuitive design. I will use the established components like `NumberInput` and `SliderInput`, imported from `inputs` in `PlannerTab.tsx`, ensuring consistency.


**Integrating Full Year Option**

I'm expanding the operating days options to include "365일 (연중무휴)" alongside "주 5일" and "주 6일" within the segmented control. The corresponding monthly days will be 30.4, 21.7, and 26.1, respectively.  I'm verifying that these presets align with the calculations and the UI element. I am also making sure to add a new option value in the UI to support the requested update.

**Expanding Day Selection**

I've just broadened the scope of the operating days selector. Now, the user will be able to choose between "주 5일", "주 6일", and "365일 (연중무휴)", with corresponding monthly values of 21.7, 26.1, and 30.4 days. I'm focusing on ensuring data integrity and accurate display within the UI element and calculations. I've updated the selector's options to accurately reflect these choices.

To satisfy your request, I will update `components/PlannerTab.tsx` to include an "Operating Days" selector in the Cafe's "Sales/Turnover Prediction" card. This will allow you to easily toggle between **5 days a week**, **6 days a week**, and **365 days (year-round)** operation modes.

Here are the changes:

entries.description: Add Operating Days selector (5 days/week, 6 days/week, 365 days) to Cafe Detail settings
